title: activity1 report draft
body:
# FINS3666 Assessment 1 Activity 1 - Analysis Report Draft

## 3.1 Data Processing I processed `/Users/yinyiping/Desktop/UNSW/FINS 3666/A1 Activity 1/IRE.X 20250115 Stocks Trades.csv` and `/Users/yinyiping/Desktop/UNSW/FINS 3666/A1 Activity 1/IRE.X 20250115 Market Depth Legacy x 10.csv`. Filtering rules followed the technical specification: only `Type=Trade` for the trade feed, only `Type=Normalized LL2` for order-book snapshots, and only continuous trading observations from 10:10:00 to before 16:00:00 (local market time). Cleaning then removed missing or non-positive price/size observations and crossed top-of-book records (`L1-AskPrice < L1-BidPrice`).

After filtering and cleaning, the pipeline retained 5953 trade records and 30261 order-book records. The as-of matching step linked each trade to the most recent order-book state at or before the trade timestamp within the same venue (`#RIC`), producing 5953 one-to-one matched observations without venue cross-contamination.

## 3.2 Market Microstructure Analysis For each matched observation, the analysis computed cumulative trade volume, trade count, trade value and VWAP, plus order-book spread, spread in bps, depth, minimum depth, and L1 order imbalance. Four mid-price methodologies were generated: simple top-of-book midpoint, volume-weighted midpoint, spread-crossing volume-weighted midpoint, and minimum-depth weighted midpoint.

Microstructure charts (`output/figures/*.png`) show intraday co-movement between traded prices, VWAP, and bid/ask quotes, and capture how cumulative order flow evolves through the session. This supports the expected interpretation that trade execution relative to bid and ask boundaries embeds useful information about short-horizon price pressure.

## 3.3 Statistical Analysis Model evaluation used MAE, MSE, RMSE, TE, correlation, and linear regression (Trade Price as dependent variable, each Mid Price variant as predictor). Results were computed venue-by-venue and jointly. Key ranking outcomes were: - IRE.AX: lowest RMSE is 0.00545 using Simple Mid Price, with MAE 0.00448, correlation 0.99526, and regression R² 0.99054. - IRE.CHA: lowest RMSE is 0.00477 using Simple Mid Price, with MAE 0.00369, correlation 0.99544, and regression R² 0.99090.

Across both venues, Simple Mid Price delivered the best overall RMSE (0.00527) with correlation 0.99527 and R² 0.99055.

Rolling-window regression with a 100-trade window was applied from the first valid window to the last pre-close trade, yielding time-varying estimates of slope, intercept and R². This provides a stability check against over-reliance on any single full-sample estimate.

## 3.4 Critical Reflection Strengths: the workflow is fully reproducible, uses strict venue-consistent matching, and evaluates multiple market microstructure proxies under a unified error/regression framework. It also includes a rolling diagnostic that better reflects changing intraday conditions.

Limitations: the model is univariate and does not use qualifier-level microstructure information (e.g., trade condition flags, crossing types, off-market filtering). It also assumes a fixed rolling window length and equal weighting through time.

For real-world deployment, improvements should include qualifier-aware data conditioning, adaptive or exponentially weighted windows, richer multivariate predictors (depth shape, imbalance dynamics, lagged returns), and more advanced models such as recursive least squares or machine-learning architectures.
